description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from a pale, stone-like material and features a single, broad archway supported by two robust square pillars. Each pillar is adorned with decorative reliefs, including floral motifs and sculpted details near the base. The top of the arch is capped with a rectangular entablature, and two ornamental elements resembling laurel wreaths or shields rest atop the structure. The surface has a slightly marbled appearance, with subtle yellowish veins running through the stone. The overall design is symmetrical and classical, evoking the grandeur of Roman or Neoclassical architecture.
description: A pale stone triumphal arch with a single archway, decorative reliefs, and two ornamental elements on top, featuring classical architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full archway, both pillars with decorative reliefs, and the two ornamental elements on top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the structure and the profile of the ornamental elements, with minimal detail visible on the pillars.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the archway and pillars, but with less visible detail on the reliefs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the thickness of the arch and the side profile of the top ornaments.
